old_claim.note: Cartography (Mercator Projection) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Trigonometry supplies the triangle-side and angle relationships used to compute unknown positions from measured baselines and angles in triangulation surveying.

source_shape.source_locator: The overview defines surveying triangulation as determining a point from known points at the ends of a baseline by measuring angles and using trigonometry rather than measuring the distance directly.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the actual computational method of triangulation as angle measurement over triangles solved with trigonometry.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This directly supports trigonometry as the enabling mathematical method, while Mercator projection is a later map-projection technique rather than a prerequisite for triangulation measurement.

ontology_before: Triangulation Surveying depended directly on Mercator Projection, conflating map projection with the triangle-solving survey method.
ontology_after: Triangulation Surveying depends on trigonometry for the angle-and-baseline mathematics used to compute unknown positions.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: triangulation_surveying no longer depends on cartography_mercator_projection and now depends on trigonometry as an enabling method prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to Mercator-projection map production rather than baseline-and-angle surveying.
A source showed triangulation surveying specifically depends on the Mercator projection rather than trigonometry.
The trigonometry node were narrowed away from triangle side-angle relationships used in surveying.
short_reason: Triangulation is solved with trigonometry; Mercator projection is the wrong dependency layer.
